name: AbsorbBench-Video (Track A redistributed shards)
description: Track A redistributed shards of AbsorbBench-Video: KuaiRand-Pure (CC BY-SA 4.0; 2,622,668 short-form video interactions, ships canonical is_rand indicator and two psi variants psi_S0 and psi_S3) and KuaiRec (CC BY-SA 4.0; 4,676,570 forced-exposure short-form video interactions; the ShareAlike obligation is preserved at the deposit level). AbsorbBench-Text (Tenrec QK-article spine) is distributed separately via the build-from-source Track B pipeline due to license incompatibility between CC BY-SA 4.0 (KuaiRec) and CC BY-NC 4.0 (Tenrec).
citeAs: Anonymous (2026). AbsorbBench (Track A redistributed shards): KuaiRand-Pure + KuaiRec parquet with per-item engagement coefficient psi (Version 1.0.0) [Data set]. DOI redacted for double-blind review.

distribution.name: kuairand_pure.parquet
distribution.description: KuaiRand-Pure (CC BY-SA 4.0): 2,622,668 short-form video (u, i, t) interactions on Kuaishou with per-row engagement coefficient psi pre-computed; also ships psi_S3 under the joint-marginal S3 = -log_2 P(tag, log10(video_duration_ms) bucket) per paper Appendix D.4.

distribution.name: kuairec.parquet
distribution.description: KuaiRec (CC BY-SA 4.0): 4,676,570 forced-exposure short-form video (u, i, t) interactions on Kuaishou with per-row engagement coefficient psi pre-computed.

recordSet.name: kuairand_pure
recordSet.description: One row per (u, i, t) tuple in the KuaiRand-Pure shard.

field.name: user_id
field.description: Namespaced user identifier.

field.name: item_id
field.description: Namespaced item identifier.

field.name: modality
field.description: short_video for this shard.

field.name: dataset_source
field.description: Source dataset label (kuairand_pure).

field.name: timestamp
field.description: Encounter timestamp (parquet logical type timestamp[ms]; values are unix-epoch milliseconds).

field.name: position
field.description: Within-session encounter position.

field.name: c
field.description: Engagement-time fraction T_engaged / T_total in [0, 1].

field.name: R_count
field.description: Per-(u, i, t) reflective-act count, integer >= 0.

field.name: K_raw
field.description: Pre-floor per-item content surprisal in bits (paper notation: S_raw).

field.name: K
field.description: Floored per-item content surprisal max(K_raw, 1) in bits. Paper notation: S = max(S_raw, 1).

field.name: R_norm
field.description: Reflective intensity in [0, 1] = min(R_count, R_p90_modality) / R_p90_modality.

field.name: psi
field.description: Per-encounter engagement coefficient c*(1+R_norm)/K, bounded in [0, 2].

field.name: S3_raw
field.description: Pre-floor per-item content surprisal under the joint marginal S3 = -log_2 P(tag, log10(video_duration_ms) // 0.5 bucket), in bits. NaN for items with missing tag or duration metadata.

field.name: S3
field.description: Floored per-item content surprisal max(S3_raw, 1) in bits. Cold-start-meta items (S3_raw NaN) use the 1-bit floor; psi_S3 is then c*(1+R_norm).

field.name: psi_S3
field.description: Per-encounter engagement coefficient under the joint-marginal S3 (tag x log-duration bucket); see paper Appendix D.4. psi_S3 = c * (1 + R_norm) / S3, bounded in [0, 2].

recordSet.name: kuairec
recordSet.description: One row per (u, i, t) tuple in the KuaiRec shard.

field.name: user_id
field.description: Namespaced user identifier.

field.name: item_id
field.description: Namespaced item identifier (renamed from KuaiRec source column video_id; prefix kr_ added for cross-shard uniformity).

field.name: modality
field.description: short_video for this shard.

field.name: dataset_source
field.description: Source dataset label (kuairec).

field.name: timestamp
field.description: Encounter timestamp (parquet logical type timestamp[ms]; values are unix-epoch milliseconds).

field.name: position
field.description: Within-session encounter position.

field.name: c
field.description: Engagement-time fraction T_engaged / T_total in [0, 1].

field.name: R_count
field.description: Per-(u, i, t) reflective-act count, integer >= 0.

field.name: K_raw
field.description: Pre-floor per-item content surprisal in bits (paper notation: S_raw).

field.name: K
field.description: Floored per-item content surprisal max(K_raw, 1) in bits. Paper notation: S = max(S_raw, 1).

field.name: R_norm
field.description: Reflective intensity in [0, 1] = min(R_count, R_p90_modality) / R_p90_modality.

field.name: psi
field.description: Per-encounter engagement coefficient c*(1+R_norm)/K, bounded in [0, 2].

rai:dataCollection: Source datasets KuaiRand-Pure and KuaiRec were downloaded from their official portals (https://kuairand.com, https://kuairec.com). The AbsorbBench build pipeline computes psi per the closed-form expression and writes per-shard parquet files with sha256-stable manifests. Source dataset time frames: KuaiRand-Pure ~30 days, April 8 to May 8, 2022 (log_standard_4_08_to_4_21 standard-exposure split + log_random_4_22_to_5_08 random-exposure split; per kuairand.com); KuaiRec ~62 days, July 5 to September 5, 2020 on Kuaishou (per Gao et al. 2022, KuaiRec paper §3 / kuairec.com). AbsorbBench performs only post-hoc computation on already-public data and does not collect new human-subject data.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Per-modality signal extraction maps source columns to a unified schema. Engagement-time fraction c = play_time / video_duration. Reflective intensity R_norm = min(R_count, R_p90_modality) / R_p90_modality, capped at 1. Content surprisal S(i) = -log_2 P_m(tag(i)) with a 1-bit floor (R1). psi(u, i, t) = c * (1 + R_norm) / S(i), bounded in [0, 2].
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: No human annotation was performed. psi is a closed-form computation over per-(u, i, t) signals available in the source datasets.
rai:dataAnnotationPlatform: N/A. psi is computed by a deterministic pipeline; no annotation platform is involved.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: N/A. psi is deterministic; no inter-annotator agreement applies.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. Source datasets are de-identified by the publishers (KuaiRand and KuaiRec). User and item identifiers are namespaced opaque tokens; no demographic, behavioural, or content information that could re-identify users is retained.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Net-positive for transparent recommender-system research: makes per-item engagement-quality estimation accessible to researchers without proprietary platform logs. Risk: uncritical operationalisation of psi as a deployment objective could reinforce engagement-maximising platform behaviour. We recommend pairing the corpus with separate platform-specific validation before any deployed-system application.
rai:dataBiases: Ranker-served selection bias: the encounter set on KuaiRand-Pure is determined by the platform's ranker. KuaiRand-Pure ships a random-exposure subset alongside the ranker-served slice; cross-slice persistence (F4 in the paper) bounds this confounding.

Forced-exposure bias on KuaiRec: KuaiRec is a fully-observed every-user-by-every-item interaction matrix obtained by directing users to a specific feed for a fixed period. This is closer to randomised exposure than typical observational logs but introduces a different bias: users were aware they were participating.

Platform and language scope: both shards are from Kuaishou (short-form video), serving primarily Chinese-language users in 2022. psi distributions may not generalise to other platforms, languages, or eras.

Tag-distribution bias in S(i): per-modality content surprisal is computed under the source dataset's empirical tag marginal. Items in different distributional contexts would receive different S values.
rai:dataLimitations: Two platforms (Kuaishou KuaiRand-Pure + KuaiRec), one language (Chinese), one modality (short-form video) for this Track-A deposit. The corpus's text/news modality (Tenrec QK-article) is shipped as a build-from-source pipeline, not redistributed here. Cross-modality numeric psi comparison requires modality-relative normalisation and is not supported by this Track-A deposit alone.
rai:dataUseCases: Per-item engagement-coefficient prediction across three split protocols (held-out items, users, time).
Cold-start estimation: psi is well-defined and informative on items with few or zero training-set encounters, where count-based popularity is rank-degenerate.
Engagement-quality vs engagement-volume calibration: psi distinguishes per-item engagement-quality from raw count statistics.
Counterfactual evaluation using KuaiRand-Pure's random-exposure slice.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Maintained by the authors via the public issue tracker on the anonymous review mirror at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/absorbbench-C138/ (full GitHub URL released upon de-anonymisation). Updates that change psi values (e.g., new S(i) estimators) bump the major version and ship as a separate Zenodo deposit; the prior version remains accessible via the Zenodo concept-DOI (DOI redacted for double-blind review). Errata tracked in ERRATA.md.
